title: Strengthening Laboratory Networks with PCR Technology for Foodborne Pathogen Testing
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Foodborne     diseases     continue     to     be     a     significant   global   health     challenge  ,   impacting     millions     of     people     annually     and     causing     substantial     economic     losses  . Rapid   and     accurate     detection     of     foodborne     pathogens     is     critical     to     prevent     outbreaks     and     protect     public     health  .   Polymerase     Chain     Reaction   (PCR)   technology   has   emerged   as   a     gold     standard     for     pathogen     detection     due     to     its     sensitivity  ,   specificity  ,   and     speed  .   Strengthening     laboratory     networks     with   PCR   capacity     allows     for     standardized   testing,   early     detection  ,   and     coordinated     responses  .   This     policy     brief     highlights     the     urgent     need     for     integrating   PCR   technology     into     national     food     safety     surveillance     systems  ,   ensuring     equitable     access     to     diagnostic     tools     across     regions  ,   and     enhancing     workforce     skills     to     optimize   data   quality  ,   strengthen     outbreak     investigations  ,   and     safeguard     food     security     at     a     national   level.
